alifornia, Berkeley, CA 94720 November 23, 1998 This paper has been superseded by Technical Memorandum UCB/ERL M01/11, March 6, 2001, also entitled Overview of the Ptolemy Project ABSTRACT The Ptolemy project studies heterogeneous modeling and de

lemy project studies heterogeneous modeling and design of concurrent systems. The focus is on embedded systems, particularly those that mix technologies, including for example analog and digital electronics, hardware and software, and electronics and

ronics, hardware and software, and electronics and mechanical devices (including MEMS, microelectromechanical systems). The focus is also on systems that are complex in the sense that they mix widely different operations, such as signal processing, f
different operations, such as signal processing, feedback control, sequential decision making, and user interfaces. Send comments to Edward A. Lee at eal at eecs berkeley edu .
